title: T-031 — API: D365 drift detection daily diff job
prompt:
Implement daily diff job per PRD §15.4 (FR-PLAN-039). Compares each suppliers row + D365-linked PO with its D365 snapshot. Tracks fields-in-drift in `d365_drift_fields` JSONB column on suppliers (add via migration). Updates `d365_sync_status='drift'` when local != D365.

Manual resolve endpoint `POST /api/planning/d365/resolve-drift` body { entity_type, entity_id, action: 'accept_local'|'accept_d365'|'merge', merged_fields? } updates accordingly + sets d365_sync_status='synced'.

RED test asserts drift detection on edit-after-pull, accept_d365 overwrites local, accept_local clears drift_fields, merge applies subset.

pipeline_inputs.description: Daily drift detection job + manual resolve endpoint with three resolution paths.
pipeline_inputs.details: Migration `0008_planning_suppliers_drift.sql` adds `d365_drift_fields JSONB` to suppliers. Diff helper compares whitelisted fields (name, address, contact_*, payment_terms, currency). Job runs daily via cron. Manual resolve only allowed when d365_sync_status='drift'.
acceptance_criteria: Given a supplier whose name was edited locally after the last D365 pull, when drift-detect runs, then d365_sync_status='drift' and d365_drift_fields contains 'name'.
Given action='accept_d365' on a drifted supplier, when resolve runs, then local fields are overwritten with D365 snapshot and d365_sync_status='synced'.
Given action='accept_local', when resolve runs, then d365_drift_fields={} and d365_sync_status='manual'.
Given `pnpm vitest run src/server/jobs/__tests__/d365-drift-detect.test.ts`, when run, then 0 failures.
test_strategy: RED tests for each resolve path + detection of drift + idempotency.
risk_red_lines: Do not allow resolve on non-drifted rows.
Do not lose audit history — log every resolution to outbox.
